title: Primitive Types 6
description: You can also access tuple elements by their index using dot notation.
instructions: Use tuple indexing to access the second element.
hints: Tuple elements are accessed with dot notation and a zero-based index.
The first element is `.0`, the second is `.1`, etc.
explanation: Tuple elements can be accessed by index using dot notation: `tuple.0`, `tuple.1`, etc. This is different from array indexing which uses square brackets. Tuple indexing must use literal integers - you cannot use a variable as the index because each position may have a different type.
